extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is violated by the interaction_drop rule, which has the highest priority (100) and sets acuity to the LOWEST value (self_care) precisely when dizziness becomes true (in combination with back_pain).

Baseline input (witness): back_pain=true, dizziness=false, pallor=false, abdominal_pain=false.
- Only raise_back_pain (priority 2) matches: acuity := urgent. interaction_drop does not fire (requires dizziness). No default needed. Result = urgent.

Now set dizziness=true, all else unchanged: back_pain=true, dizziness=true, pallor=false, abdominal_pain=false.
- raise_back_pain (priority 2) matches -> urgent.
- interaction_drop (priority 100) matches (back_pain AND dizziness) -> self_care.
- The matching rule with the highest priority wins: priority 100 > priority 2, so interaction_drop wins. Result = self_care.

In the listed enum order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], self_care is strictly lower than urgent. So flipping dizziness false->true lowered acuity from urgent to self_care, contradicting the requirement that setting dizziness true never lowers acuity. The property is violated.
